title: Investigate intermittent missing Astro assets on production
description: Production sometimes renders without CSS after the EmDash to Astro standalone cutover. Browser console reports 404 for hashed /_astro assets referenced by a post HTML page. Investigate repository build output, deployment configuration, and live HTTP/cache behavior to identify the likely root cause before changing code.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN investigating the production no-CSS symptom, THE analysis SHALL distinguish missing deployed assets from client/network-only failures. WHEN evidence is collected, THE report SHALL cite concrete repository or HTTP observations. WHEN no code fix is applied, THE issue SHALL remain open or be updated with next action.
close_reason: Investigation completed: production currently serves HTML referencing current assets, but reported errors reference old Astro asset hashes that now 404. The slash redirect is explained by Cloudflare Static Assets default HTML handling for folder/index.html and is related to canonical URL behavior, while the no-CSS symptom itself is caused by stale HTML / asset generation skew during or after deployments.

title: Consolidate lefthook biome step to 'biome check' to stop the lint/format race
description: biome lint --write adds blank lines after the opening --- in .astro frontmatter; biome format --write removes them. lefthook.yml runs both in parallel with stage_fixed:true, so the order their writes land in is non-deterministic and sometimes silently reverts staged style fixes (producing empty commits or extra blanks). Replace the two-step pre-commit with a single 'biome check --write' invocation to apply lint+format+assist atomically, matching the pnpm run lint:fix / fmt scripts and biome's own pre-commit recommendation.
close_reason: shuymn_me-xpc: feat shipped (commit 432ec19) — /posts/<slug>.md emits 23 ja routes, en endpoint ready for future projection. shuymn_me-9gz: lefthook biome step consolidated to 'biome check' (commit e6be821); follow-up sweep landed in 5c2ea8b. Verified by re-running commit with new config: biome reported 'No fixes applied' on already-clean files instead of fighting itself.

title: Collapse post source to root posts
description: Owner clarified that slug is derivable from the extensionless filename and should not be stored in author source, metadata JSON should be removed, and canonical post source should live at root posts/*.md. Generate Astro frontmatter into src/content/posts/ja/*.md and remove content/source/posts plus content/metadata/posts.
design: Update project-local-content to read posts/*.md with title-only frontmatter, derive slug from filename, derive publishedAt from date-prefixed filename, and generate minimal SEO frontmatter deterministically. Remove one-off recovery/init modes now that historical source recovery is complete. Update content files, Astro projection, docs/ADR/AGENTS, and tests. Do not run deployment commands.
acceptance_criteria:
- When author source is stored, it shall live at posts/<slug>.md without a locale directory.
- When author source is parsed, slug shall be derived from the extensionless filename and shall not be required in frontmatter.
- When projection runs, src/content/posts/ja/<slug>.md shall be generated with Astro frontmatter including locale, slug, publishedAt, title, and generated SEO data.
- When local content checks run, no content/metadata/posts JSON files shall be required.
- When project-local-content runs, it shall support only projection check/apply plus source/projection directory overrides, not one-off recovery/init modes.
- When verification completes, no deployment command shall have been run.
close_reason: Completed. Canonical post source now lives at root posts/*.md with title-only frontmatter; slug is derived from the extensionless filename; content/source/posts and content/metadata/posts JSON sidecars were removed; src/content/posts/ja/*.md is regenerated projection with slug, locale, publishedAt, title, and deterministic SEO frontmatter; one-off recover/init modes were removed from project-local-content. Verification: pnpm run fmt, pnpm run lint, pnpm run test:local-content, pnpm run project:content -- --check, pnpm run typecheck, pnpm run build, bd lint --json, git diff --check. No deployment command was run.

title: Simplify local post metadata contract
description: Trim accepted post metadata to fields that remain durable publishing inputs after owner feedback. Move slug into author source, derive publishedAt from the date-prefixed slug, remove top-level description, updatedAt, draft, visibility, redirects, and revision from accepted metadata, and keep SEO metadata as the accepted description surface.
design: Update scripts/project-local-content.mjs, source and metadata fixtures, Astro content schema, public components/routes, and the local-source design docs. Keep src/content/posts as the generated Astro projection. Do not run deployment commands.
acceptance_criteria:
- When local content is projected, slug shall come from author source and match the source filename.
- When local content is projected, publishedAt shall be derived from the leading YYYY-MM-DD segment of the slug.
- When accepted metadata is parsed, it shall reject removed fields: description, publishedAt, updatedAt, draft, visibility, redirects, and revision.
- When pages render post metadata, SEO description shall drive page meta and top-level description shall not be displayed.
- When verification completes, no deployment command shall have been run.
close_reason: Completed metadata contract simplification. Slug now lives in author source and must match filename; publishedAt is derived from the date-prefixed slug during projection; accepted metadata rejects slug, description, publishedAt, updatedAt, draft, visibility, redirects, and revision; SEO description remains under seo; public pages no longer render top-level descriptions or filter on draft metadata. Verification: pnpm run fmt, pnpm run lint, pnpm run test:local-content, pnpm run project:content -- --check, pnpm run typecheck, pnpm run build, bd lint --json, git diff --check. No deployment command was run.

title: Move blog canonical sources outside src
description: Owner wants the blog canonical source outside src. Move author source and accepted metadata out of src/content while keeping Astro projection under src/content/posts. Also codify that deployment is human-only and agents stop at dry-run.
close_reason: Moved canonical blog author source and accepted metadata outside src to content/source/posts and content/metadata/posts while keeping src/content/posts as the Astro projection. Updated project-local-content defaults, ADR/design docs, README, AGENTS, and gitignore. Removed the production deploy script and codified that agents must not run wrangler deploy or pnpm run deploy without explicit owner authorization. Verification: pnpm run fmt, pnpm run lint, pnpm run test:local-content, pnpm run project:content -- --check, pnpm run typecheck, pnpm run build, bd lint --json, git diff --check. No deployment was run.

title: Define local source metadata projection contract
description: Define and implement the local content contract required by ADR 0003 before the EmDash-free cutover can be deployable. The target is not rich Markdown frontmatter. Author source, accepted metadata, generated state, and the Astro build projection must be separate so the normal writing flow can start from title and body while generated editorial metadata remains inspectable and accepted before publication.
design: Start from docs/adr/0003-adopt-astro-only-local-markdown-public-path.md and docs/design/blog-platform.md. Choose the smallest source format that keeps title/body easy for Coding Agents and local editors. Store durable metadata in sidecars or another local structured layer. Store suggestions/run state separately. Generate or load a projection for Astro pages/content collections. Do not require English translation files for this issue.
acceptance_criteria:
- When author source is defined, it shall contain title and body plus only the minimal publish envelope needed to keep the source addressable.
- When accepted metadata is defined, it shall live outside the author body file and shall cover durable publishing inputs such as slug, dates, tags, series, summary, SEO metadata, OGP inputs, redirects, visibility, and revision/status notes.
- When generated metadata is produced, it shall be stored as generated state and shall not affect public pages until accepted or deterministically promoted.
- When Astro renders public routes, it shall consume a regenerable projection from author source plus accepted metadata rather than treating hand-authored rich frontmatter as the source of truth.
- When the contract is implemented, docs and tests shall record the chosen file layout, parsing rules, projection rules, and validation evidence.
- When this issue completes, no English generated post files shall be required.
close_reason: Completed local-source contract implementation: added docs/design/local-source-contract.md, split 22 Japanese posts into author source and accepted metadata sidecars, added project:content apply/check projection command and tests, removed generated-state frontmatter from the Astro post schema, and verified pnpm run fmt, pnpm run lint, pnpm run test:local-content, pnpm run project:content -- --check, pnpm run typecheck, pnpm run build, and git diff --check.

title: Rebaseline remaining blog-platform issues for Markdown author source
description: After ADR 0003 and the owner clarification, several open blog-platform baseline issues still contain old implementation assumptions such as cache hints, plugin surfaces, Portable Text headings, CMS-side telemetry, or rich-frontmatter authoring. Before those issues are executed, rewrite or supersede them against the Astro author source target.
design: Review open blog-platform issues that mention CMS-specific implementation boundaries or assume all metadata lives in post frontmatter. For each, either update design/acceptance to the author source / generated state / Astro projection model, supersede it with an author source issue, or close it if the capability is no longer wanted. Keep this as backlog hygiene only; do not implement the features here.
acceptance_criteria:
- When this issue runs, it shall inspect open blog-platform issues for CMS-specific design, acceptance, labels, required skills, Portable Text storage, cache hints, plugin writes, and rich-frontmatter assumptions.
- When an issue remains valid, it shall be rewritten to target the author source / generated state / Astro projection contract rather than CMS runtime, plugins, cache hints, Portable Text storage, or generated metadata embedded in author source.
- When an issue is obsolete, it shall be superseded or closed with a replacement rationale.
- When rebaselining completes, bd ready shall not show feature work that still requires the old CMS comparison model for the replacement site.
- When English generation issues are rebaselined, they shall remain post-cutover follow-ups unless the owner explicitly brings translation back into the deployment gate.
notes: Rebased after owner feedback: backlog hygiene must remove old CMS assumptions, rich-frontmatter assumptions, and comparison-oriented wording. Current vocabulary is author source, generated state, and Astro projection.

title: Recover Japanese canonical posts from git history
description: Japanese-only Markdown source files exist in git history under _posts/ and posts/. Before the local-source cutover is deployable, recover those historical Markdown bodies as the canonical Japanese author source instead of treating EmDash-exported Markdown as final truth. Current evidence: git log shows 22 historical post files under _posts/ and posts/, matching the current Japanese post count.
design: Inspect historical commits for _posts/*.md and posts/*.md, map each historical file to the current Japanese public slug, and recover title/body into the author-source layer defined by shuymn_me-1er.16. Preserve public slugs and publication/update dates through accepted metadata rather than requiring all durable metadata to live in post frontmatter. Do not change English generation or image handling in this issue.
acceptance_criteria:
- When historical posts are recovered, each current Japanese post shall be mapped to a historical Markdown source or explicitly reported as unmatched.
- When a historical source exists for a current Japanese post, the canonical author body shall come from the historical Markdown file rather than the EmDash export.
- When title or body differs between historical source and EmDash export, the issue shall record the intentional reconciliation rule before replacing source.
- When durable metadata is preserved, it shall be stored through the accepted metadata contract rather than relying on rich post frontmatter as the source of truth.
- When recovery completes, validation shall record the git commits or paths used and any intentional differences from the EmDash export.
notes: Rebased after owner feedback: this issue now recovers Japanese author source for the source/metadata/projection contract instead of preserving a rich frontmatter contract.
close_reason: Recovered 22 Japanese canonical author-source posts from bbcd28c4ed5148db1422179a082fffc95ef4c29f^:_posts/*.md, preserved durable publishing fields through accepted metadata sidecars, recorded git-history reconciliation notes, regenerated Astro projections, documented the mapping and normalization rules in docs/design/japanese-source-recovery.md, and verified pnpm run fmt, pnpm run lint, pnpm run test:local-content, pnpm run project:content -- --recover-from-git 'bbcd28c4ed5148db1422179a082fffc95ef4c29f^' --check, pnpm run project:content -- --check, pnpm run typecheck, pnpm run build, bd lint --json, and git diff --check.

title: Export existing EmDash content to local Markdown
description:
Before public routes can safely cut over to Astro local Markdown, export the existing local EmDash content from data.db into repository-local Markdown files. Current local evidence: data.db contains 22 published ja posts, 22 published en posts, and published home-about site sections for ja/en, while seed/seed.json contains no content rows. This issue converts that content into the local canonical source format without solving image upload.

## Required Skills
building-emdash-site
design: Use the existing EmDash Portable Text to Markdown conversion path where possible. Export posts to src/content/posts/<locale>/<slug>.md with strict frontmatter aligned to ADR 0003. Preserve title, description, slug, locale, publishedAt, updatedAt, draft=false for published posts, generation metadata, and body Markdown. Export or separately model home-about only if needed for the first public route cutover. The script should support dry-run and deterministic output; it must not modify data.db.
acceptance_criteria:
- When the export runs in dry-run mode, it shall report the number of posts and site sections it would export without writing files.
- When the export writes posts, it shall create one Markdown file per published EmDash post under src/content/posts/<locale>/<slug>.md.
- When exported posts are validated, their frontmatter shall satisfy the local post content collection schema.
- When Portable Text cannot be represented as Markdown without opaque blocks, the export shall fail with the affected slug and leave the output incomplete rather than silently losing content.
- When home-about content is needed for the public route cutover, the issue shall either export it to a local source file or record why it is handled by a separate issue.
- When export completes, the issue shall record counts, commands, and validation evidence.
notes:
Export completed: pnpm run export:emdash-content -- --dry-run reported 44 published posts (ja 22, en 22), 2 published site sections, and 44 output files; pnpm run export:emdash-content -- --apply wrote src/content/posts/<locale>/<slug>.md. Validation: pnpm run test:emdash-export, pnpm run test:english-generation, pnpm run fmt:fix, pnpm run lint, pnpm run typecheck, pnpm run build, and git diff --check. Build passed with pre-existing EmDash/session warnings from the separate spike route; the generated posts collection itself validated.
Follow-up during public route cutover: export CLI was extended to site_sections and rerun with --apply --force. Final export shape is 44 post files plus 2 site-section files under src/content/site-sections/<locale>/home-about.md, 46 files total.
close_reason: Export completed: deterministic CLI exported 44 published EmDash posts to src/content/posts, preserved table blocks through the existing Markdown bridge, and validation passed.

title: Complete atomic cutover to Astro local source
description: Complete the replacement of the EmDash-backed blog implementation with an Astro local-source architecture. This is not a staged deployment: Japanese public rendering, the source/metadata/projection contract, historical Japanese Markdown recovery, and removal of EmDash runtime/scripts/dependencies/environment requirements all belong to the deployable cutover boundary. English translation content and generation are not part of this deployment because English translations have not been applied in production; revisit them after the EmDash-free site is deployed.
design: Atomic full cutover, not a staged deploy. The deployable target is Astro local source, not rich frontmatter as the primary metadata store. Completion requires author source, accepted metadata, generated state, and build projection to be separated; Japanese public rendering must consume that local projection; historical Japanese Markdown must be reconciled from git history; EmDash runtime/admin/deploy/export/generation dependencies must be removed from the deploy target; docs/ADRs must stay aligned. Do not close this issue while any EmDash script, dependency, environment requirement, Portable Text storage boundary, or hand-authored rich-frontmatter workaround remains necessary for normal posting or deployment. Do not require en/*.md or English generation for this cutover.
acceptance_criteria:
- When the local content contract is implemented, author source shall contain title and body plus only the minimal publish envelope needed to keep the source addressable.
- When accepted metadata is stored, it shall live outside the author body file and shall be inspectable before generated values affect public pages.
- When public Japanese post routes render, they shall preserve /posts/<slug> and consume the local source/metadata projection rather than EmDash runtime reads.
- When static paths are generated, they shall come from local Japanese projected entries and shall not rely on empty global i18n route duplication.
- When the build runs, public local-source Japanese post routes shall prerender without EmDash request/session warnings caused by those routes.
- When Japanese canonical posts are finalized, they shall be reconciled from historical Markdown files in git history or explicitly reported as unmatched.
- When the cutover is considered deployable, no English generated post files shall be required; generated English Markdown may be absent until translation work is restarted after deployment.
- When the cutover is considered deployable, no EmDash scripts, seed files, plugin code, package dependencies, environment variables, or docs shall remain in the deploy target except explicitly archived historical ADR context.
- When implementation changes approved scope or design decisions, docs/design/blog-platform.md and the related ADR shall be updated in the same task.
notes:
Public route migration completed: exported 44 posts and 2 home-about site sections to local Markdown; home and post detail rendering now use Astro content collections; Astro config no longer installs the EmDash integration; deleted EmDash live config/generated env and obsolete spike route; static default OGP moved to /og-default.svg. Verification: pnpm run export:emdash-content -- --dry-run reported 46 exportable files, pnpm run lint, pnpm run typecheck, pnpm run test:emdash-export, pnpm run test:english-generation, pnpm run build, git diff --check. Build prerendered 44 public post pages with no EmDash request/session warnings. Remaining English generation storage migration split to shuymn_me-2jj.
Correction after owner feedback: staged migration is not an acceptable deployment plan. The current branch state proves local Markdown public rendering, but it is not deployable while EmDash scripts/dependencies, EmDash-backed English generation, or EmDash-derived canonical Japanese bodies remain unresolved. Treat the previous closure as premature; completion now requires an atomic cutover with no EmDash/local-Markdown coexistence in the deploy target.
Updated design/acceptance after owner clarification: completion is an atomic full cutover. The current local Markdown rendering work remains useful evidence, but it is not a deployable end state until shuymn_me-2jj and shuymn_me-gjp are complete and EmDash deployment/runtime/migration leftovers are removed from the target.
Owner clarified that English translations are not in production and should be revisited after the EmDash-free site is deployed. Removed shuymn_me-2jj as a blocker and made it depend on the atomic cutover instead; generated src/content/posts/en/*.md files are not required for this cutover.
Owner feedback corrected the frontmatter assumption: the deployable target must separate author source, accepted metadata, generated state, and build projection. Created shuymn_me-1er.16 as the blocker for defining and implementing that contract; English files remain out of scope for this cutover.
close_reason: Completed atomic cutover to Astro local source: local source/metadata/projection contract is implemented, 22 Japanese posts were recovered from git history, public home and Japanese post routes prerender from Astro content collections, EmDash runtime/scripts/seed/patches/dependencies/environment variables and old Portable Text/English-generation storage boundary were removed from the deploy target, Cloudflare adapter/deploy scripts were added, and Cloudflare deployment succeeded. Verification: pnpm run fmt, pnpm run lint, pnpm run test:local-content, pnpm run project:content -- --check, pnpm run typecheck, pnpm run deploy:dry-run, git diff --check, bd lint --json, pnpm run deploy. Cloudflare Worker deployed as shuymn-me version 9f1db089-19e5-4cb1-b89f-8e6132e3a235; verified 200 for https://shuymn-me.shuymn.workers.dev and https://shuymn.me plus rendered Hello World post content.

title: Decide Astro-only or supporting CMS path after local Markdown spike
description: The local Markdown prototype confirmed that Astro can prerender a sample post while the project remains output:server, but it also exposed two migration constraints: the current Astro i18n setup attempts localized variants for the spike route, and the current EmDash integration reads Astro.request/session during prerendered routes. If local Markdown becomes the canonical source, EmDash should be removed from the public content path rather than preserved as a long-term runtime integration. This issue tracks the next platform decision: Astro alone, or Astro plus a supporting CMS/editor layer that does not own the canonical post body. Image upload and asset storage remain out of scope.
design: Evaluate an explicit locale-aware route strategy for local Markdown posts and compare Astro-only against Astro plus a supporting CMS/editor layer. Treat EmDash as a transitional dependency to remove if local Markdown is adopted. Keep the work scoped to routing and platform boundary decisions; do not migrate all posts, build an editor UI, or solve image upload here.
acceptance_criteria:
- When local Markdown routes are introduced beyond the spike, they shall generate one deterministic public URL per content locale and slug without relying on empty global i18n duplicates.
- When a local Markdown public route is prerendered, the build shall complete without EmDash request/session warnings caused by that static route.
- When local Markdown is selected as the canonical post source, the target architecture shall remove EmDash from the public content path instead of preserving it as a runtime dependency.
- When a supporting CMS/editor layer is proposed, it shall not own the canonical post body and shall explain how local Markdown remains the source of truth.
- When the chosen boundary changes the platform direction, docs/design/blog-platform.md and the relevant ADR shall be updated in the same task.
close_reason: Completed platform decision after the local Markdown spike: ADR 0003 adopts Astro-only local Markdown as the next public-content target, defers supporting CMS/editor layers, treats EmDash as transitional, and opens shuymn_me-1er.14 for the public route migration slice.

title: Evaluate local Markdown canonical source spike
description:
Evaluate whether shuymn.me should move the canonical post source from EmDash/Sanity-style cloud or database content to local Markdown-family files that Coding Agents can edit directly. The spike is motivated by agent-native drafting, reviewable diffs, local ownership of drafts, and avoiding Portable Text as the primary authoring and translation boundary. Image upload and external asset storage are explicitly out of scope for this spike and should not block the content-source decision.

## Required Skills
building-emdash-site
design: Compare local Markdown, MDX, and Markdoc as canonical post formats. Prefer the smallest spike that proves authoring, frontmatter contracts, English generation boundaries, Astro rendering, and SSG/SSR fit. Do not remove EmDash implementation during the spike. Record the decision pressure against Sanity and EmDash, especially Portable Text, cloud-canonical storage, plugin/API boundary cost, and future maintenance cost. Keep image and media upload as a deferred follow-up.
acceptance_criteria:
- When the spike starts, it shall document the candidate local canonical-source architecture and the exact questions it must answer.
- When the candidate format is evaluated, it shall include Markdown-family choices and explain why the selected first spike format is easiest for Coding Agents, translation, and deterministic validation.
- When Astro rendering is evaluated, it shall identify which routes can be SSG and which still require SSR.
- When English generation is evaluated, it shall keep LLM structured output small and avoid asking the model to emit complex document trees.
- When the spike excludes images, it shall record image upload and asset storage as a later follow-up rather than solving it now.
- When the spike changes the platform direction, docs/design/blog-platform.md and an ADR shall be updated before implementation work proceeds.
notes: Started the local Markdown canonical source spike. Added ADR 0002, a dedicated spike design document, a strict Astro content collection for local Markdown posts, one sample local Markdown post, and a noindex spike route. Validation: pnpm run fmt passed; pnpm run lint passed; pnpm run typecheck passed; pnpm run build passed and prerendered /spikes/local-posts/ja/local-markdown-canonical-source/index.html. Observed build warnings: existing EmDash integration touches Astro.request/session during prerendered routes, and Astro i18n attempts an /en variant for the spike route with no output file. These are recorded in docs/design/local-markdown-canonical-source-spike.md as migration constraints.
close_reason: Completed the local Markdown canonical source spike: documented ADR/design rationale, added strict Astro content collection and noindex sample route, validated fmt/lint/typecheck/build, and filed shuymn_me-1er.13 for the remaining route/i18n and EmDash integration boundary work.

title: Implement host-side EmDash schema/content deployment script
description:
Implement the first host-side deployment/migration script for EmDash schema and deployable content configuration. The script should make ongoing remote updates possible without treating seed/seed.json as the only post-deployment production reflection mechanism.

This is the local-first step before any CD integration. The script should be runnable against local EmDash through dev-bypass and against any selected remote EmDash instance through the same baseUrl/token/header connection model.

## Required Skills
building-emdash-site,emdash-cli
design: Use the shared host-side connection helper from shuymn_me-1er.9. Start with the smallest deployable surface that is useful for this repo: schema/collection fields and non-post configuration that can be represented through EmDash APIs. Do not add local/prod target modes. Do not write directly to D1 or SQLite unless the EmDash API cannot express a required operation and that limitation is documented.
acceptance_criteria:
- When the script runs with --dry-run, it shall compare desired deployable state with the selected EmDash instance and print the planned changes without writing.
- When the script applies changes, it shall use the same baseUrl/token/dev-bypass/header connection model as other host-side scripts.
- When run against local EmDash, it shall be able to verify changes against the local SQLite-backed instance without Cloudflare resources.
- When a requested operation cannot be expressed through the current EmDash API, the script shall fail with a clear unsupported-operation result instead of silently editing seed files or direct database state.
- When changes are applied, the script shall verify the returned EmDash state and be safe to re-run without duplicating schema or configuration.
- When the implementation changes the host-side automation contract, docs/design/blog-platform.md and docs/adr/0001-blog-platform-direction.md shall stay aligned.
notes: Implemented host-side EmDash deploy script. Rationale: use the shared URL/token/dev-bypass/header connection model from shuymn_me-1er.9 and keep writes on the authenticated EmDash REST API surface instead of treating seed/seed.json as a production reflection mechanism or editing SQLite/D1 directly. The first supported deployable surface is settings plus collections/fields because EmDash exposes API operations for settings, collection create/update, field create/update, and field reorder. Seed content, destructive deletions, and immutable field type changes are intentionally unsupported results for this slice. Impact scope: new pnpm run deploy:emdash CLI, shared EmDash API client helper, schema/settings planner and applier, package scripts, AGENTS command list, docs/design/blog-platform.md, and docs/adr/0001-blog-platform-direction.md. Verification: Red test failed on missing deploy module, then pnpm run test:emdash-deploy passed; pnpm run test:english-generation passed; pnpm run deploy:emdash -- --help passed; with local Astro/EmDash dev server running, pnpm run deploy:emdash -- --dry-run --dev-bypass returned ok=true with zero changes and unsupported=[]; pnpm run deploy:emdash -- --apply --dev-bypass returned ok=true and verification changes=[]; pnpm run fmt, pnpm run lint, pnpm run typecheck, and pnpm run build passed.
close_reason: Completed host-side EmDash deploy script for settings and collections/fields with dry-run/apply verification, docs updates, and tests.

title: Extract shared EmDash host-side connection helper
description:
Create a shared connection layer for host-side EmDash automation scripts so generate:english, schema/content deployment, and future maintenance scripts use one consistent endpoint-driven model.

The helper must not introduce local/prod target modes. It should resolve an EmDash base URL, API token, dev-bypass flag, and generic request headers from environment variables and matching CLI options. This follows the existing generate:english pattern and the host-side automation boundary documented in docs/design/blog-platform.md and docs/adr/0001-blog-platform-direction.md.

## Required Skills
building-emdash-site,emdash-cli
design: Prefer a small TypeScript module under src/lib or scripts shared by host-side scripts. Preserve the existing generate:english behavior while moving duplicated connection parsing into the helper. Generic headers should support Cloudflare Access service-token headers without making Cloudflare Access a special production mode.
acceptance_criteria:
- When a host-side script needs an EmDash client, it shall create it through the shared helper.
- When connection options are resolved, the helper shall support EMDASH_BASE_URL / --base-url, EMDASH_API_TOKEN / --token, EMDASH_DEV_BYPASS / --dev-bypass, and generic headers from environment or CLI flags.
- When generate:english is migrated to the helper, its current CLI behavior and tests shall remain equivalent.
- When neither token nor dev-bypass is configured, the helper shall fail fast with an actionable error.
- When Cloudflare Access headers are supplied, the helper shall attach them without hardcoding local/prod environment names.
notes: Implemented shared host-side EmDash connection helper and migrated generate:english to use it. Rationale: keep the endpoint/credential-driven model consistent across scripts and avoid local/prod branches; use Gunshi for declarative script option definitions while preserving strict unknown-option failures and JSON error output for non-help failures. Impact scope: scripts/generate-english-posts.ts CLI parsing/client creation, new scripts/lib host CLI and EmDash connection helpers, EMDASH_HEADERS + --header/-H contract, docs/env metadata, and focused tests. Verification: pnpm run test:english-generation, pnpm run fmt, pnpm run lint, pnpm run typecheck, pnpm run build, pnpm run generate:english -- --help, and node scripts/generate-english-posts.ts --dry-run.
close_reason: Completed shared EmDash connection helper, Gunshi CLI commonization, docs/env updates, and verification.

title: Run generate:english CLI against production via Cloudflare Access
description:
Make the existing pnpm run generate:english CLI usable against the production EmDash deployment by going through Cloudflare Access, then run a real production translate->review->write cycle to validate the end-to-end pipeline before deciding whether a separate Cloudflare Worker is needed.

Per docs/adr/0001-blog-platform-direction.md the CLI is "designed for local runs, cron, or a future Worker trigger". This issue exercises the local-run side of that contract first; the future Worker trigger remains a fallback if CLI proves operationally inadequate.

Background: an earlier attempt to call EmDash through Cloudflare Access from the host failed due to redirect behavior. EmDash 0.9.0 now ships emdash/client/cf-access with isAccessRedirect, customHeadersInterceptor, getCachedAccessToken, and runCloudflaredLogin helpers, which should make the path tractable.

## Required Skills
building-emdash-site,emdash-cli
design:
Prefer the emdash@0.9.0 cf-access module rather than rolling custom auth in the CLI. Two viable paths:
- CF Access service token (CF-Access-Client-Id/CF-Access-Client-Secret) injected via customHeadersInterceptor + EMDASH_HEADERS env var. Non-interactive, suited for cron/CI later.
- Cached cloudflared JWT via getCachedAccessToken with runCloudflaredLogin as the bootstrap step. Tied to a human identity, suited for one-off local runs.

Start with the service token path because the documented automation boundary is host-side automation, not interactive use; cloudflared remains as a fallback if the service-token redirect bug recurs.

If 0.9.0 still emits a redirect that the client helpers cannot resolve, fix forward inside the EmDashClient interceptor stack rather than papering over it in the CLI argv layer.

Idempotency relies on the existing classifyExistingEnglishCandidate logic in src/lib/englishGeneration.ts (source_version + content_hash). The first production run shall be --dry-run --limit 1 against a single --source slug before broader runs.
acceptance_criteria:
- When the CLI is run with configured Cloudflare Access credentials, it shall reach the production EmDash REST API and complete a translation pipeline run without manual browser interaction.
- When the cf-access auth flow encounters a redirect that the emdash 0.9.0 client helpers cannot resolve, the CLI shall fail fast with a diagnostic identifying the redirect target instead of silently following it.
- When at least one production translate->review->write cycle succeeds end to end (after --dry-run validation against a single source), the issue shall record the exact command, the result summary, and any observed operational limitations.
- When measured production reliability or operational cost proves insufficient (manual auth refresh frequency, redirect failures, throughput limits, or CPU/network limits hit), a follow-up issue shall be opened to evaluate the standalone Cloudflare Worker path described as a future trigger in docs/adr/0001-blog-platform-direction.md.
- When the cf-access integration changes the documented automation path or its operational expectations, docs/adr/0001-blog-platform-direction.md and docs/design/blog-platform.md shall be updated in the same task.
notes:
Decision rule for opening the Worker follow-up: defer until concrete operational evidence demands it. Possible triggers include repeated cf-access auth failures, the need for unattended scheduled runs that local cron cannot serve cleanly, or pipeline runtimes that exceed reasonable host-side execution budgets.

Out of scope: building the Cloudflare Workflow Worker itself, service-binding wiring, or any plugin migration. Plugin migration is explicitly ruled out by docs/adr/0001-blog-platform-direction.md (lines 56-62) because the standard 0.9.0 content:write surface cannot set slug, locale, translationOf, or publishedAt.

title: Fix /en post links generating duplicated locale prefix
description: HomePage.astro と PostArticle.astro が getPostPath に entry.id を渡しており、英語ロケールでは entry.id が en/<slug> 形式のため /en/posts/en/<slug> が生成される。post.data.slug に切替える。
close_reason: HomePage.astro と PostArticle.astro で getPostPath に post.data.slug を渡すよう変更し、英語ロケールでの二重プレフィックスを解消。slug nullable に対する型ガードも追加。/en, /, 各記事ページで 200 確認。typecheck/lint パス。

title: Implement gated English auto-publication
description:
Implement baseline slice 3 English generation: automatically generate English versions for eligible published Japanese posts, run separate automated translation review plus deterministic checks, auto-publish passing results with a fixed visible translation note, and keep failed generations unpublished with failure reasons.

## Required Skills
creating-plugins,building-emdash-site
design: Default is gated auto-publication, not routine human approval. Use the verified write path from the automation boundary issue for locale, slug, and translationOf writes. The Japanese source remains authoritative.
acceptance_criteria:
- When a Japanese post is published and not explicitly excluded, the system shall create or refresh a source-versioned English generation candidate unless a deterministic precheck rejects unsupported content.
- When translation and review run, prompts shall treat the Japanese post body as source data, not as executable instructions.
- When generated English content passes automated translation review and deterministic checks, the system shall publish it with slug, locale, translationOf/source linkage, preserved code blocks, preserved links, recorded gate results, and a visible note rendered from a fixed site template.
- When generated English content fails checks, the system shall keep it unpublished and expose the failure reason without blocking the Japanese publication flow.
- When the Japanese source changes, regeneration shall be explicit and shall not silently overwrite an edited English version.
- When quality, failure rate, or operational noise proves insufficient, the implementation shall document evidence before adding a mandatory human review gate.
close_reason: Implemented gated English auto-publication baseline with host-side EmDash REST automation, automated review/deterministic gates, failure metadata, fixed translation note rendering, schema fields, docs, and verification.

comments.author: Shu YAMANI
comments.text: Rationale: use a host-side EmDash REST automation path because the verified standard plugin API cannot write locale, slug, publish state, and translationOf together. Impact scope: posts schema gains English generation state fields; generated English posts get automated gate metadata; English post rendering shows a fixed translation note; docs/ADR describe the command as the baseline triggerable path. Verification: pnpm run seed, dev-server typegen for emdash-env.d.ts, pnpm run generate:english -- --dev-bypass --dry-run --limit 1, pnpm run test:english-generation, pnpm run fmt, pnpm run lint, pnpm run typecheck, pnpm run build.

comments.author: Shu YAMANI
comments.text: Follow-up: runtime testing showed the generated English body must stay Portable Text end to end. Updated the implementation to read raw EmDash Portable Text, require Portable Text before provider calls, request structured contentPortableText, store Portable Text directly, derive Markdown only for checks/hashes, and stop the run on unexpected post-level errors before later LLM calls. Verification: pnpm run test:english-generation; pnpm run typecheck; pnpm run fmt; pnpm run lint; pnpm run generate:english -- --dev-bypass --dry-run --limit 2; pnpm run build.

comments.author: Shu YAMANI
comments.text: Follow-up: English generated posts now publish and repair publishedAt from the Japanese source instead of using generation time. Date-only drift is repaired without LLM calls and is excluded from source-version regeneration decisions. Verification: pnpm run test:english-generation; pnpm exec biome check scripts/generate-english-posts.ts scripts/generate-english-posts.test.ts src/lib/englishGeneration.ts docs/adr/0001-blog-platform-direction.md docs/design/blog-platform.md; pnpm run typecheck; pnpm run generate:english -- --limit 1 --dry-run --dev-bypass.

comments.author: Shu YAMANI
comments.text: Follow-up: source precheck no longer rejects EmDash table blocks as opaque content. Table blocks are rendered as Markdown tables for translation, translated Markdown tables are converted back to EmDash table blocks, prompts instruct table preservation, and deterministic checks reject table count drift. Verified against 2020-12-22-renewal: pnpm run generate:english -- --source 2020-12-22-renewal --limit 50 --dry-run --dev-bypass returned dry_run_create. Additional verification: pnpm run test:english-generation; pnpm exec biome check scripts/generate-english-posts.ts scripts/generate-english-posts.test.ts src/lib/englishGeneration.ts docs/adr/0001-blog-platform-direction.md docs/design/blog-platform.md; pnpm run typecheck.

title: Implement deterministic locale-aware OGP images
description:
Implement baseline slice 3 OGP work: post-specific OGP images generated from durable post metadata, locale-aware English/Japanese OGP routes, versioned image URLs, static fallback behavior, and Cloudflare-runtime proof for the selected renderer.

## Required Skills
building-emdash-site
design: Do not use image-generation AI. Compose a repo-owned base image and accepted text such as title, optional tag or series, locale, and site identity. If dynamic PNG rendering needs unsupported Node/native/font/media behavior on Cloudflare, generate cacheable assets ahead of time through a host-side service or CLI.
acceptance_criteria:
- When a post is shared externally, the system shall provide a post-specific OGP image generated from durable post metadata.
- When a localized post route is shared, the system shall provide an OGP image for that locale-specific path.
- When a title wraps, the renderer shall use locale-aware segmentation and shall not split English words, Japanese word segments, code identifiers, or URLs mid-token.
- When a single unbreakable token cannot fit, the renderer shall use controlled font scaling or a deliberate overflow fallback instead of arbitrary mid-word breaking.
- When metadata changes, the generated OGP URL shall include a stable version component so social caches can observe the update.
- When the renderer is selected, local and Cloudflare runtime verification shall be recorded before it becomes the primary og:image path.

title: Implement thought continuity surfaces
description:
Implement baseline slice 2 from docs/design/blog-platform.md: series support, automated series suggestions, series navigation, deterministic related posts, optional status notes, and generated table of contents for long posts.

## Required Skills
building-emdash-site,creating-plugins
design: Keep embedding-based related posts adaptive. Baseline related navigation must be deterministic and inspectable. Use site-native rendering for public surfaces and plugin-backed suggestions only where they reduce non-writing editorial toil.
acceptance_criteria:
- When a post belongs to an ongoing line of thought, the author shall be able to accept, reject, or adjust a suggested series.
- When a reader views a series member, the page shall expose navigation to neighboring or related series entries.
- When related posts are shown, the system shall derive them from deterministic signals such as shared tags, series, or recency before using embeddings.
- When a post is long-form, the page shall expose a table of contents derived from rendered Portable Text headings.
- When a post is exploratory, outdated, or superseded, the author shall be able to expose an optional status note without changing the post body.

title: Implement recall backbone
description:
Implement baseline slice 1 from docs/design/blog-platform.md: tag taxonomy, tag archive pages, automated tag suggestion path, search entry point, RSS/sitemap verification, consistent published/updated dates, and a simple year/month archive.

## Required Skills
building-emdash-site
design: Prefer site-native schema, Astro pages, and components for public surfaces. Use building-emdash-site patterns: server-rendered EmDash content, cache hints, entry.id for slugs, and seed/seed.json as the schema/content seed source.
acceptance_criteria:
- When a post has accepted tags, the system shall expose tag archive pages that list matching posts and preserve EmDash cache hints.
- When a reader wants to find prior thinking, the system shall expose a search page or compact search entry point before relying on external search engines.
- When RSS or sitemap output is generated, the repository shall include a verification path that proves the expected public URLs are present.
- When a post is rendered, the page shall show publication and update dates consistently.
- When schema or seed content changes, the seed/bootstrap flow needed to prove the schema loads shall be run and recorded.

title: Verify EmDash automation write boundaries
description:
Verify which parts of the strategy can be implemented through standard EmDash plugins and which require site-native Astro routes, host-side APIs, CLI automation, trusted first-party extensions, or standalone Cloudflare Workers. This is the blocker for durable metadata writes, localized English content writes, taxonomy relationship writes, and OGP generation path selection.

## Required Skills
creating-plugins,building-emdash-site
design: Use creating-plugins before plugin work. Standard plugins may own hooks, review state, scoped storage, admin UI, constrained HTTP calls, and verified content writes. Durable localized writes, infrastructure bindings, long-running jobs, and public cacheable binary routes must move outside the standard plugin when the verified API cannot express the contract.
acceptance_criteria:
- When a standard EmDash plugin API is considered for a durable write, the current installed API shall be verified against the required fields or relations.
- When the plugin API cannot express slug, status, locale, translationOf, taxonomy relation, media, or public binary route requirements, the issue shall document the smallest non-plugin implementation boundary.
- When verification is complete, the issue shall record the commands, inspected files, and API evidence used for the decision.
- When the design decision changes the approved boundary, docs/design/blog-platform.md and docs/adr/0001-blog-platform-direction.md shall be updated in the same task.
notes: Verification completed. Evidence inspected: package.json emdash 0.9.0; node_modules/emdash/src/plugins/types.ts; plugins/context.ts; plugins/hooks.ts; api/schemas/content.ts; api/handlers/content.ts; astro/routes/api/content/[collection]/index.ts; astro/routes/api/content/[collection]/[id]/publish.ts; astro/routes/api/content/[collection]/[id]/terms/[taxonomy].ts; plugins/routes.ts; astro/routes/api/plugins/[pluginId]/[...path].ts; cli/commands/content.ts; cli/commands/taxonomy.ts. Decision: standard plugin can own hooks, review state, scoped storage/KV, constrained HTTP, media operations, and field/SEO content writes when content:write is enough. Current standard plugin write API cannot express slug/status/locale/translationOf/author/byline/publishedAt/publish state or taxonomy relation writes; English auto-publication, accepted taxonomy assignments, and OGP public binary delivery require host-side/API/CLI/trusted-extension/Worker boundaries as documented. Docs updated: docs/design/blog-platform.md and docs/adr/0001-blog-platform-direction.md. Verification commands passed: pnpm run fmt, pnpm run lint, git diff --check, bd lint --json.
close_reason: Completed EmDash 0.9.0 automation write boundary verification and documented the plugin/API/CLI/Worker decision with evidence.

title: Blog platform baseline implementation
description:
Implement the memorandum-first blog platform baseline defined in docs/design/blog-platform.md and docs/adr/0001-blog-platform-direction.md. This epic exists to track execution only in bd; the design docs and ADR remain the source of truth for scope and trade-offs.

Scope includes the recall backbone, thought continuity surfaces, deterministic OGP generation, gated English auto-publication, maintenance feedback, and Cloudflare-first telemetry. Adaptive follow-ups should stay reversible and evidence-driven.
design: Source of truth: docs/design/blog-platform.md and docs/adr/0001-blog-platform-direction.md. Use EARS acceptance criteria. Use bd only for task tracking.
acceptance_criteria:
- When baseline work is planned, each child issue shall map to a named baseline slice or explicit adaptive follow-up in docs/design/blog-platform.md.
- When implementation changes approved scope or design decisions, the related design doc and ADR shall be updated in the same task.
- When non-trivial implementation work is completed, the issue shall record rationale, impact scope, and verification evidence from relevant commands.

title: lefthook sync strips '.beads/hooks/pre-commit' BEADS INTEGRATION block when lefthook.yml changes
description: core.hooksPath is set to .beads/hooks, so both lefthook and beads write to the same pre-commit script. beads appends a 'BEGIN BEADS INTEGRATION ... END BEADS INTEGRATION' block after lefthook's bootstrap. When lefthook detects a config change it regenerates the hook from its own template, dropping the bd-managed block silently. Repro: edit lefthook.yml and commit; lefthook auto-sync rewrites .beads/hooks/pre-commit and the bd block disappears. Mitigation observed today by reverting via git restore. Long term, decide whether to (a) move lefthook hooks back to .git/hooks (override hooksPath inside lefthook config), (b) chain beads' integration via lefthook's commands instead of appending to the script, or (c) wrap bd hooks run in a separate file invoked by lefthook.

title: Add /posts/<slug>.md raw Markdown endpoint
description: Mirror the suzuuuuu09.com mechanism: appending .md to a post URL returns the raw Markdown so LLM crawlers and humans can grab the source. Add static endpoints for both ja (/posts/<slug>.md) and en (/en/posts/<slug>.md). Output is a minimal frontmatter block (title only) plus the post body, served as text/markdown; charset=utf-8. Implementation: Astro endpoint files src/pages/posts/[slug].md.ts and src/pages/en/posts/[slug].md.ts using getStaticPaths from getCollection('posts') filtered by locale, prerender = true, returning new Response(\"---\\ntitle: ...\\n---\\n\\n\" + entry.body).
close_reason: shuymn_me-xpc: feat shipped (commit 432ec19) — /posts/<slug>.md emits 23 ja routes, en endpoint ready for future projection. shuymn_me-9gz: lefthook biome step consolidated to 'biome check' (commit e6be821); follow-up sweep landed in 5c2ea8b. Verified by re-running commit with new config: biome reported 'No fixes applied' on already-clean files instead of fighting itself.

title: Rename local post schema
description: Rename content.config.ts localPostSchema because the site no longer has local/remote post variants; keep the Astro posts collection schema name aligned with the current Astro-only source model.
close_reason: Completed: renamed content.config.ts localPostSchema to postSchema and updated the posts collection reference. Also applied formatter-required removal of blank lines after Astro frontmatter in four existing files so fmt/lint gates pass. Verified with fmt, lint, typecheck, build, and git diff --check.

title: Add rendered spacing to home about paragraphs
description: Home about copy now has explicit paragraph markup, but Tailwind preflight removes default paragraph margins. Add CSS so the blank paragraph break after the B2B SaaS sentence is visible in rendered Japanese and English pages.
close_reason: Completed: added explicit .about paragraph margins so rendered home/about paragraphs have 16px visual gaps. Verified build output with agent-browser against static dist: Japanese paragraph gaps [16,16,16], English paragraph gaps [16,16,16,16], plus fmt, lint, typecheck, build, and git diff --check.

title: Make home about copy JSX-like markup
description: Replace HomePage about string arrays with explicit locale-branch markup so paragraph breaks are visible in the component source, including a blank paragraph break after the B2B SaaS sentence in both Japanese and English.
close_reason: Completed: replaced the HomePage about string arrays with locale-branch Astro markup, keeping explicit paragraph breaks after the B2B SaaS sentence in both Japanese and English. Verified with fmt, lint, typecheck, project:content check, astro build --force to refresh stale Vite cache, normal build, rendered dist checks, bd lint, and git diff --check.

title: Remove site sections collection and rename post component
description: Replace EmDash-era site-sections content collection with locale-specific HomePage constants, remove the site-sections collection/files, and rename LocalPostArticle to a non-local/non-duplicative component name.
close_reason: Completed: removed the EmDash-era siteSections content collection and src/content/site-sections files, moved home/profile copy into HomePage locale constants with shared profile links, renamed LocalPostArticle to PostPage, updated docs/ADR/AGENTS, and verified with fmt, lint, typecheck, local-content tests, project:content check, build, bd lint, and git diff --check.

title: Rebuild English generation after author source cutover
description: After the EmDash-free Japanese author source site is deployed, rebuild English generation against the author source / generated state / Astro projection contract. English translations are not part of the current deployment gate because they have not been applied in production. This follow-up should read Japanese author source, generate translated author source plus translation metadata, keep generated state separate, and preserve the existing deterministic table/code/link checks without reintroducing EmDash or Portable Text as the storage boundary.
design: Start after shuymn_me-1er.14 and shuymn_me-1er.16 are complete. Reuse the prompt/review/deterministic-check lessons from src/lib/englishGeneration.ts and scripts/generate-english-posts.ts, but change the storage boundary to Markdown author source, generated state, and Astro projection. Structured Output should stay small: translated fields and Markdown body, not a large document tree. Do not treat rich frontmatter as the primary generated metadata store.
acceptance_criteria:
- When the command runs in dry-run mode, it shall read eligible Japanese author source without calling EmDash.
- When the command applies a generated translation, it shall write or update English author source through the author source / generated state / Astro projection contract.
- When generated translation state is recorded, it shall remain separate from author source until gates pass or the author explicitly accepts it.
- When source content contains Markdown tables, code fences, or URLs, deterministic checks shall preserve the existing table/code/link guarantees.
- When an English source file has unmanaged or manually edited values, the command shall not overwrite them unless an explicit force/regenerate flag permits that behavior.
- When this migration completes, the English generation path shall have no EmDash API/client imports, EmDash environment requirements, Portable Text storage boundary, or generated-metadata-in-frontmatter dependency.
notes: Post-cutover follow-up after owner clarification: English translations are not in production and should be rebuilt after the EmDash-free author source deployment. This issue depends on shuymn_me-1er.14 and should not block the current cutover.

title: biome scripts に import sort (assist) チェックを統合
description: biome format / biome lint は assist actions (organizeImports) を実行しないため、CLI で import sort が検査されない。VSCode 拡張のみ source.organizeImports.biome として動作している。package.json の lint/fmt スクリプトを biome check ベースに切り替え、CI で import sort を検査できるようにする。
close_reason: package.json の lint/lint:fix/fmt/fmt:fix スクリプトを biome check ベースに切り替え完了。biome.json のスキーマ URL も 2.4.14 に更新。commit: dfc3941

title: Wire Cloudflare-first telemetry interpretation
description: Implement or document the baseline telemetry path where Cloudflare remains the source of truth for raw traffic, status-code, cache, bot, and performance signals, and EmDash only receives derived editorial aggregates when they are useful.
design: This is infrastructure telemetry, not content source data. Prefer Cloudflare-native analytics, host-side scripts, scheduled jobs, standalone Workers, or another Cloudflare-native workflow over EmDash pageview collection.
acceptance_criteria:
- When readership signals are reviewed, Cloudflare-provided telemetry shall be treated as the primary source of truth.
- When EmDash receives telemetry-derived data, it shall receive only derived aggregates needed for editorial interpretation, not raw first-party pageview collection.
- When credentials are needed, they shall remain in environment or Cloudflare bindings and outside public or plugin-owned state.
- When a standalone Cloudflare Worker is chosen, the issue shall document why the work is infrastructure-side rather than plugin-side.
- When Cloudflare-native views are enough, no EmDash-side reporting surface shall be added.

title: Implement maintenance feedback
description:
Implement baseline slice 4 maintenance feedback: automated publish checklist, internal link integrity checks, redirect rules for changed slugs or imported legacy URLs, and verification reporting that fits the memorandum-first blog strategy.

## Required Skills
building-emdash-site,creating-plugins
design: Keep the first implementation focused on correctness and maintenance. Use static redirect config or Cloudflare rules first; add EmDash admin/plugin surfaces only if manual maintenance becomes frequent.
acceptance_criteria:
- When a post is prepared for publication, the system shall expose an automated checklist derived from site requirements without turning SEO into keyword-score chasing.
- When internal post links are present, the repository shall provide an automated check that detects broken internal links.
- When a URL changes after publication, the system shall provide an explicit redirect path instead of silently losing old links.
- When maintenance automation writes state, it shall use the verified plugin or private automation boundary and record enough evidence to debug stale or duplicate generated state.

title: Evaluate CD wrapper for host-side EmDash deployment
description:
After the host-side schema/content deployment script has been proven locally and against an explicitly selected remote EmDash instance, decide whether and how to wrap the same operation in CD.

This issue exists to prevent premature Cloudflare preview or GitHub Actions yak shaving while preserving the follow-up path once manual script operation is reliable.

## Required Skills
building-emdash-site,emdash-cli
design: Do not replace the host-side script with a separate production-only workflow. CD should call the same script with configured base URL, EmDash API token, and generic Cloudflare Access headers when needed. Evaluate GitHub Actions first because it can manage setup, secrets, and command output explicitly; Cloudflare Workers Builds remains an option only if it can express the required resource and secret handling cleanly.
acceptance_criteria:
- When the host-side script has not been proven manually, this issue shall remain blocked.
- When CD is evaluated, it shall call the same script and shall not introduce separate local/prod code paths.
- When Cloudflare Access protects _emdash/*, the CD design shall include service-token headers or another non-interactive Access path.
- When preview deployment is considered, the design shall explicitly account for D1/R2/KV/secrets not being automatically cloned per PR.
- When CD is not worth the operational cost, the issue shall record that decision and keep manual script execution as the supported path.

title: Evaluate adaptive follow-ups after baseline usage
description: Track reversible post-baseline decisions that should not be implemented until real usage produces evidence: embedding-based related posts or semantic clustering, mandatory human review for English translations, EmDash-side Cloudflare aggregate reporting, newsletter, ActivityPub, alternate LLM providers, or standalone Worker implementations used only for operational reasons.
design: Adaptive capabilities must remain reversible. Add them only when maintenance burden, quality, or recall/distribution value justifies the extra surface area after baseline usage.
acceptance_criteria:
- When deterministic related navigation is sufficient, embedding-based related posts shall not be added.
- When English auto-publication quality and pipeline reliability are sufficient, mandatory human review shall not be added.
- When Cloudflare-native analytics views are sufficient, EmDash-side reporting shall not be added.
- When publishing cadence does not justify distribution overhead, newsletter and ActivityPub shall remain unimplemented.
- When an adaptive feature is added, the issue shall record observed evidence, removal criteria, and the smallest implementation boundary.
